label: Gaussian processes

node.label: Gaussian processes
node.definition: A Gaussian process places a probability distribution over functions and uses a kernel to couple observations, interpolate predictions, and quantify posterior uncertainty.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Gaussian processes is modeled as depending on Kernels & implicit feature maps, Bayesian & approximate inference and feeding downstream nodes such as Time-series forecasting, anomaly & fraud detection, Scientific ML, simulators & digital twins, Weather, climate & geospatial forecasting.
examples: atlas downstream link to Time-series forecasting, anomaly & fraud detection
atlas downstream link to Scientific ML, simulators & digital twins
atlas downstream link to Weather, climate & geospatial forecasting
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Kernels & implicit feature maps, Bayesian & approximate inference.

dependencies.rationale: Gaussian processes is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Kernels & implicit feature maps.

In this atlas graph, Gaussian processes has an `optimizes_for` dependency on Bayesian & approximate inference because Bayesian & approximate inference is modeled as the objective family for learning, selection, or evaluation.
Gaussian processes consumes, emits, or organizes information using Distributions, uncertainty & beliefs.
